reviews.review_description:
Update:

I hadn't played it for a long while and between now and then navigation became glitchy.

I get frequently stuck in doorways. (No, I haven't put on weight. 😁)

I'm running it on the Quest 3 with no QGO or ADB tweaks.

Original Review:

This is the third platform I've experienced The Manor on, I also have it for Go and PlayStation VR. The fact that several other reviewers have purchased this multiple times should give you an indication of how highly it's regarded.

Affected: The Manor is like owning your own Halloween attraction. It's a walk through a haunted mansion with multiple paths to the end, not a horror adventure game. How long each path takes depends on how long you spend cowering in the dimly lit corridors gathering the strength of will to slowly open the next door.

I've only gone through one path so far but I can say that It's easily the best version I've played. Most of it was all new, and the situations that were familiar were so much better than in the Go or PSVR version. It's the best VR haunted house I've ever experienced. Unlike the Go and PSVR versions you can now interact with objects and open doors. (I hope the developers update this to use hand tracking. Maybe you could point with a finger to move forward?)

The art direction and design are top notch all the way through. As to be expected the limitations of the Quest's processing power can be seen but there are some areas that rival PlayStation VR quality. The developers are pushing the Quest hard.

I'd like to address the complaint other reviewers have about the graphics smearing in dark areas. This seems to be a hardware issue and other games on the Quest with dark areas suffer from it. For example the dark areas in Star Wars Vader Immortal smear and blur as you look around. My guess is that the only thing the developers could do to address this would be to turn up the brightness, which would be a shame. Haunted mansions should be dimly lit.

The technical issue I hope the developers do look into is the movement. If you lean towards something to get a closer look it often knocks you back, breaking the immersion. The developers have already released a patch that addresses complaints about turning, so they're responsive to user feedback. (I just stand and turn around to navigate the Mansion. That's one of the perks of untethered VR, no smooth or click turning necessary.)

As much as I love VR horror, I would not recommend this or any other horror title for introducing people to VR for the first time. It would be the equivalent of introducing someone to hot sauces with a bottle of ghost pepper sauce. VR horror can be a very unpleasant experience for some people.

For anyone who has some experience with VR and is interested in horror, this is a must buy.

And on the title page there's a "Coming Soon" notice. :)

To the Affected Manor born.
This app just smokes and smokes and smokes and smokes and smokes and smokes. And smokes.
Top class, excelsior, superior graphics. On the level of profound. To say that this app is a Dom Perignon, Mercedes Benz app that runs on all 12 cylinders would be an understatement. Are most people aware of how incredible this VR app is?
Affected Manor is even better than the haunted houses that amusement parks run. Just as movies could do things that live theatre couldn't, VR could do things that amusement park haunted houses couldn't do such as placing their hand anywhere at any point on a wall and that point opens into a hole wide enough to walk through that stays open for a minute and then closes itself like a person would find on an alien UFO. VR can replicate that experience virtually whereas human technology won't be able to do that for about a hundred years.
Thank you for Affected Manor. This is an example of how life can often be good to those who are good people.

This app is 100 stars out of 10. Profound. If this app was a restaurant dish, it would be no less than a Michelin 3 star dish. Chef's kiss. Excelsior. Magnificent. Visceral. Life changing. Next level.

This app is a gift from Satan. And I mean that in the best way possible.

The game has everything needed to be the greatest horror vr game, but then comes the hitbox, I’ve never seen so bad made hitboxes in my life, the objects you see have their hitbox 4 feet away from the texture, which breaks the immersion in it, easy to fix the problem but stills on the game

reviews.review_title: Graphic Upgrade on the girl and doll
reviews.review_description:
The game is very good , however. There should be a graphic upgrade. The ghost girl and the doll seems a bit cartoony. 

Next thing is to add random moments where the ghost girls walks behind you, shodows past by you, you shee ghost girl watching you from afar in the dark, you may hear things from falling behin, or beside, you have to turned  around, walks by you.... Water dripping from roof to floor,

Other ghost entities that stand still and don't move... And then disappears when you get close.

Just had more jump sacres moments. 

And add more game modes! A mode to walk through the cemetery, or back yard would be nice! Something like Stay alive Movie!

I did the gauntlet and each time it's buggy. First time the place on the floor disappear and you can't continue. You just float over the empty floor.

Is there no way to get back to the main hall without quitting and reloading? 

Second time I didn't even make it as far. A door closed and can't be opened to continue. 

Third time I tried the normal mode. Boring. 

The game says it measures how much you screen. Not once did I ever. There's nothing scary about this at all.
Don't waste your time and money on this. It's highly overrated and unentertaining at best. Think of it as going through a very lame haunted maze with VERY limited interaction

This is one of a handful of games I use to show off the Oculus Quest to people for the first time and it's really entertaining in a group setting just to watch someone play and laugh at their reactions.

The setting is a classic Victorian haunted house. Objects move on their own, doors open and slam behind you, ghosts make an appearance, etc. The graphics are quite good in terms of what the Quest is capable of. The visuals can look a bit washed out or grainy because of the dark setting, but the last update seems to have boosted the brightness and improved this substantially. 

It's basically a haunted house simulator. It's not really a game as much as it is an experience. In terms of scariness, there were a few jump scares that got me but they are fairly spaced out. I would say they could have crammed more scares in but I've also seen many people refuse to play more than a few minutes.

You use the joysticks to move around and motion to aim the flashlight and open doors. This makes it very easy for a beginner to jump in, although the free locomotion may set off some people more sensitive to motion sickness. It is mostly linear except a couple points where the path branches. If you run through the game, it's about 15 minutes or so, but if you take every path that's 4 playthroughs. 

That's not even counting updates that add the new "Darkness" and "Gauntlet" modes where you navigate an additional environment with only a candle or speedrun through a course while dodging objects that try and block your way. 

At 10 bucks, I would say it's worth it, however I will say I'm disappointed the PCVR version you get with cross-buy is disappointing because it used to be a completely different experience from the Quest version with higher fidelity graphics, but they ported the Quest version to PCVR version and now that original version is no longer available.

reviews.review_title: Not COMPLETE without the movie theater
reviews.review_description:
**update 10/29/2025. The "Legacy Edition" on PC is still not working with the Quest controllers and the developer just doesn't seem to care. Also, the developer indicated that their new experience "The Asylum" would release several years ago. Not sure what is going on, but I'm fairly certain that Affected the Manor is now abandonware and that Affected the Asylum will never see the light of day. Perhaps this company is dead. That's a real shame since these experiences showed so much promise.***

**update. I was referred to the "Legacy Edition" on PC which apparently includes the movie theatre. The problem (that many many many people - including me - are having is that the Oculus controllers are not binding properly when loading the Legacy Edition. There are tons of complaints about this and they are all being ignored by the developer. So the question is - why would you release a Legacy edition on PC but not properly bind the Oculus controllers - then NOT support it and help your customers? I love Affected, it is my favorite experience, but I can't give you more than one star when you don't support your Legacy edition and have left your customers hanging because the controllers don't work for it. Very disappointed in your lack of support for the Legacy edition and for not including the movie theatre on a version you claim is "COMPLETE"***

Finally, we have the Manor - Complete Version. I was so excited when I saw that Fallen Planet was releasing this version because I thought they would add the movie theater that was in the original HTC Vive / Steam version to the Quest version. 

Unfortunately, this did not happen, so I call this the "ALMOST Complete Version". 

I've owned this experience for the GearVR, Go, PSVR, Vive, and now Quest. The Manor doesn't disappoint. But I really want to see the movie theatre added back to the game. When that happens, you'll get my fifth star! ☺
When's the next update on any game y'all got going on for the quest 3

this game was a short, scary, and very fun experience. after completing everything that this game has to offer i would really recommend adding a few more experiences so this game won’t feel ended after you complete it all.
This is my go to experience at Halloween. I love it! It’s both scary and checks all the boxes for what a haunted house should be! Great job!

I got to the point where you have to walk the planks across the empty floor and you get halfway and that doesn't allow me to continue would like to get a refund but it's saying that I'm not able to get a refund so I give this game a one!

It's an old but still a very good scary experience. Good graphics, shadows, and scary sounds. Sometimes you'll actually flinch and even scream! If you want to scare your girlfriend, this is a good choice!)

Ok,  I absolutely love scary and eerie movies! I'm not far into it and I hear it's not perfect but during the trial my wife came to the room and touched my shoulder and I nearly jumped out of my skin !😱👻. When that happened I knew I'd like it so bought it straight away. The atmosphere is really creepy you wonder what is around the corner, you get glimpses of figures in the corner of your eye, the sounds,behind you, the creepy child laughing,,the doll!! This is definitely not for everyone but if you're a brave horror fan you'll love it. In Ireland as kids there was a big old abandoned house in the middle of an apple orchard and we used to go there at night and scare each other. This reminds of the thrills we had. Kudos to the development team. They really captured the atmosphere.

I bought the application because I like horror experiences and I was especially excited when I read somewhere about an update for the current Quest 3. Okay, apparently it consisted only in adding dynamic shadows, which are of course great and add to the atmosphere, BUT on the other hand, it is spoiled by blurry textures with low resolution and therefore no details (except for the girl and baby figures). At the same time, the gradual appearance of some details, sharpening, and only when the player gets completely close to the object/space (pop up?) does the impression collapse. I have the impression that the game is not well optimized, because another, much more fundamental problem is that you have "marked invisible paths" along which you are allowed to move and you get stuck in various inappropriate ways.  I've tried the game 3 times so far and each time it ended in failure in some part, when you get stuck in front of a door, while you should already be in the next area. The falling planks that you have to walk on at a height above the open area of ​​the house, are also completely unfinished. I got stuck in the middle of the area twice and can't get any further. That's quite a bummer, since there's no continuous saving and I would have to start all over again from the beginning. In the part where you're running for time, I ended up outside the door of the corridor with windows and then I managed to sneak to the right of the door, behind the windows of that corridor and just got stuck, you can't even get into the corridor with the center button on the controller. Stupid, gross mistakes that shouldn't be there at all. The idea of ​​the whole "game" itself is great.  If a new version were released just for Quest 3, in the full graphics parade that the device allows, if the engine was tuned, it would be a great horror experience and excitement for the players. I would be happy to pay for that. I'll be saving this for my little nephew, of course he'll be scared too, but I can already see the problems when it happens again somewhere and you end up off the map ☹️ The biggest shame is that the developers, despite the known bugs, which I'm not the only one mentioning, have probably completely neglected to fix and possibly update and upgrade the application.
Perfect

I've played this game I actually like it. It is scary th
ough. I jumped first time playing it.

reviews.review_title: A Blast & Must Have for Horror Fans!
reviews.review_description: I’ve played this on the PSVR before but this edition made for the Meta Quest is way better by far. I’ve only tried the standard walkthrough and “The Darkness” so far and both were fantastic with updated graphics and variations to the manors walkthrough with some mind blowing surprises. “The Darkness” had me so tense the entire time with only a candle to light your way through the dark hallways and rooms not knowing what’s lurking around the next corner. Great sound effects and creepy music help create an extremely creepy atmosphere. All horror fans needs to add this to their collection asap!!! A++
I love scary games and this one definitely game me chills! It’s nice sometimes to just have a scary experience without having to fight monsters and ghosts.
Got the game last month, doesn't work. Please fix it. Thank you.

I don’t write these. May be my first but, had such a genuine blast playing this I thought why not. They put a lot of heart (evil hearts ;) ) into this creation. Wonderful details and genuine scare the beejeezus outta me moments. The music and sights were crafted so well. I don’t want to give spoilers. So remember it’s only a game … and keep fresh pants near you. Now I need to see if I can sleep and if the hair on the back of my neck will eventually go down. Remember the dogs jumping through the window in the first resident evil? 1000 times worse the scares herein … enjoy the manor!!
The best atmospheric horror game ever!

This game can go up to 4-5 stars if the graphics get enhanced for Quest 3 users, and if the movement/smooth turn becomes normal like other games.  

The potential is there as the game is genuinely scary, and the whole haunted house/maze is rather good.
Needs updating   seems developers no interest  in this now just abandoned  it

Please give this game a new update, I’m a massive fan and would appreciate a new update or game… please 

Thank you 🙏
Walking through the halls of this game creeps me out every little detail is so realistic I played that game for 6 hours straight because it was so realistic and I actually hid from scary things

However, I wish there was more to the story. Because just when you think you understand what’s going on also, there’s more beds, there’s more pictures. I mean it kind of seems to hint at the kids being used or abused or something to that matter.

For the price this game is well worth Especially with the meta 3 update I don’t think I will use the pc version any more to show this game. If you want to play seated just change your height in the meta settings. The game is a bit old now but it’s held the test of time well I would recommend this game.

reviews.review_title: Bad first impression, but seems good.
reviews.review_description:
So I finally gave in to this haunted house walk through, and it seems very well put together. I can't say it's scary at all, but like the other "scariest" VR horror titles thatvI don'tfind scary (Exorcist, Paranormal Activity, Dreadhalls, etc), I still love the horror atmospheres and exploring these worlds. The graphics in this title beingb some best out of them all, even if it's thanks to the limitations of being an experience rather than a game.
So for the cheap price, it's a nice little 25 minute experience.

My "bad first impression" I referred to is something that made me have to stop playing. I got to the scene where the elevator you get in drops/ crashes to the lowest floor. But when I got control back, I was BEHIND the elevator, only able to walk to its sides, but unable to get in front of it to progress further into the halls. NOTHING I did got me out of it, so I had to quit after 25 minutes...

Seems nice from what I played though..

terrible updates:

but are you really kidding us? texture improvement for quest three?

and not only do I not see any improvement but I also see a worsening! 

candle light sucks! the halo around the candle light is now as if it were in low resolution!

there are also slight slowdowns in the framerate every now and then. 
(I use ovr metrics tool)
and all this in the current update that you tell me was made specifically for quest three, which has no improvements, and is actually slightly worse than the previous one! 

you didn't even increase the resolution! still 111, I at least thought you would increase it to 160 at least... But where do you see the improvement? 

give me back the old version and the five stars will return, otherwise this is the right time I delete the game from the viewer, I kept it until now to let my friends try it every now and then, but this new update was disappointing! 

on these two I should still have the game with the old update, I would really bother to compare them!...

------

for the racing mode it could be very interesting to add the possibility of playing it in "running on the spot" mode, the same mode that the Happy run app uses. using the Quest's internal accelerometers it is able to establish whether you are physically running in place and translate this into an in-game run at variable speed depending on whether you run slower or faster, this would be a perfect mode (to be added as alternative stick movement option) for the timed running game mode.
if you don't know what I'm talking about, 
you can find happyrun for free on applab by typing happyrun
This game really brings out the horror elements you would want in a VR horror game. And once you finish it, you feel very rewarded. Really amazing game
This was the best nd scary experience I had bro even my aunt was laughing when she heard me scream😭🙏🏽
I'm wheelchair bound so I can't stand as it says, it would be great if you could add sitting as a way for those of us wheelchair bound or others thats unable to stand

This game is 100% demonic.  What's with the satanic pentagrams throughout the game?  What's with the Ouija boards?  I didn't see any of that in the trailer.  This is unacceptable, and will be requesting a refund.

reviews.review_title: If you enjoy scary things, this is good.
reviews.review_description:
A nice price, not overpriced like so many games.
I like all the jump scares and won’t say anything about the content, and I’ve not gone all the way to the end yet, but will soon. 
Do another with maybe better graphics for Q3 and you’ll get my money!
Recommend it!

developer_response.body: So glad you enjoyed it, thank you for the 5 stars!

reviews.review_description: Personally, I would've liked it more if it slowly built up to the jumpscares instead of constantly following a little girl for there to be a jumpscare every few corner or hallway. There should be more suspense and more unexpected things should happen. The gauntlet's cool, though. If you like constant jumpscares, this is a good choice.
It's not optimized for Quest 3. Looks really bad. Otherwise it would be a fun little experience to show to your friends.
￼This is my first VR game system and I absolutely love it. The best thing I’ve purchased for myself in a long time money well spent ❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️

I'll start with the downside: Too short. Wished it where a bit longer.

On the other hand, thank God it was short. If it where longer I think I wouldn't keep going!!!

Love the details. Everytime I took a step I have to stop and be amazed by my surroundings!!!

And what about the sounds?.......Not a word. Play it and enjoy the ride!!!

I love this I specifically bought the oculus so I could have a scary VR and when I got it I only seen a couple and was pretty bummed so I googled about this one and thought why not it’s cheaper and seems scary, well I’m so glad I did, I was playing it the other night and made my 12 year old daughter sit in the living room with me while I played it cause I was so scared lol definitely recommend this game

reviews.review_title: Not a "game", more of an experience
reviews.review_description: I guess I didn't understand what I was buying. It wasn't a game really, it was more of an "experience". It's mainly just walking through different areas. There's not much for you to do in the experience as there are very few interactive activities. There were a few jump scares but mostly objects just moving around. It's good for a run through and maybe strapping your friends in to give them a scare but not much else. Could have been a great game if it had been more interactive and more creatures with less focus on objects shifting around.
Fun. We are new with Oculus Quest. Just right for training and evil. Will keep playing it.  Great.

Well, I had no idea what I was getting myself into. First time ever playing with VR and I jumped straight into this one. My voice is hoarse from screaming so much. I almost ruined my living room from trying to get away. Very creepy video game and great graphics! I have nothing to compare it to, but heck it’s wonderful and I’m playing it again as soon as I’m not nausea anymore. No need to do to Haunted Hose this year at Halloween!

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to leave a review...make sure you video yourself playing next time!!!

reviews.review_description: For $10 this isn't worth it

Thank you all developpers for this amazing expérience. Not another action zombie game but a true scary immersive experience in a creepy manor. I love the atmospher ! Thank you ! This is my best app on oculus so far. Hope you will add other manors or places soon, even if we have to pay for it. Your work deserve it. 
Not that fan of "the gantlet" concept. I do prefer the creepy calm and scary experience of the darkness and the manor. 
Please keep going and thanks Again  ! The horror movies dont scare me anymore, your game did !
By the way, if you're looking for a music composer, i'm your man ;-)
See you ! Waiting for your next app !
just walking through a house

developer_response.body: Hi there, we're sorry you didn't enjoy the experience, thank you for taking the time to write a review though.

reviews.review_description: Just played an hour of this and it is terrifying. I am a horror veteran and I can't think of any movie or game that has creeped me out as much as this has most of the textures and assets look very good with things like a leather sofa reflecting the light different to a painting also a tip I would be is to check the walls there is a good amount of attention to detail. I would recommend the snap turning to help with motion sickness I honestly don't know why this isn't rated better and at around 8£ I think it's a bargain

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your great review! We try our best to put out the highest quality at an affordable price so so it is great to see your comments.

its not scary and very bad , iplay it on Gear VR before and it was best VR horror games .

Once i start the game after the title comes everything turns black and thats it every other game is working i tried turning off the vr nothing changed quitting and launching the game again also didn’t help.

developer_response.body:
UPDATE: Please can you let us know if this issue has been resolved for you with the latest Oculus firmware update? It doesn't appear to be any issue with our app that has caused the crash.

Hi there, I am sorry you have experienced this issue. Please can you email info@fallenplanetstudios.com so we can assist you further. This is the first report we have had of this so we are not sure why this is the case but we will ensure it gets resolved for you.

UPDATE: Please can you get in touch with us via info@fallenplanetstudios.com so we can find out if you are still having any issues? Many thanks :)

reviews.review_description: It shuts down and goes to the oculus menu everytime I go to own the first set if sliding door. How do I get a refund?

developer_response.body: Hey there, there was an issue with the wireless casting but we have now pushed an update to fix this. Please reinstall AFFECTED: The Manor and let us know that it is working fine for you. Our email address is  info@fallenplanetstudios.com Sorry for any inconvenience caused.

Hi there,

We're really sorry you have experienced this, I have passed it onto our developers to look into. Please reach out to us via info@fallenplanetstudios.com so we can ensure your issue is resolved. Many thanks
